heroHeadline:
Khamenei Claims 'Dizzying Blow' as Trump Rejects Ceasefire;
Iran's 67th Wave Targets Israel; Switzerland Breaks with US on Arms and Airspace;
Qatar LNG Offline Day 2; Brent ~$112; 7,200+ Targets Struck
heroSubtitle: Day 22 — Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement claiming Iran had delivered a 'dizzying blow' to the enemy, prompting Trump to flatly reject ceasefire talks: 'There is no ceasefire as long as Iran refuses to surrender.' Iran launched its 67th retaliatory wave, targeting Israeli cities and US Gulf bases with ~42 projectiles; 6 civilians wounded near Rishon LeZion — no Israeli fatalities. In a dramatic diplomatic break, Switzerland halted US arms export authorizations and closed its airspace to US military flights linked to the Iran campaign, citing permanent neutrality. Trump publicly called NATO allies 'cowards' for failing to deploy Hormuz warships. Qatar LNG production remained offline for a second consecutive day, triggering an 18% Asian LNG price surge, a 22% jump in European TTF gas futures, and a UN FAO emergency food price alert. Brent crude settled near $112/bbl. US-Israel strikes brought the cumulative target count to 7,200+ with IDF now claiming 73% of Iran's mobile launchers and 87% of radar/air defense destroyed. Anti-war protests in Tehran, Isfahan, and Tabriz were suppressed by Basij forces — at least 14 arrests on Nowruz. Inside Iran, reports describe growing civilian pressure for an end to the war despite the internet blackout exceeding 310 hours.
footerNote: Data compiled from 100+ sources across 4 media poles as of March 21, 2026. All contested claims are marked. Multi-pole sourcing active.
